login.title: Sign in to printcast
login.description: Use your admin email and password.

dashboard.title: Supervision
dashboard.description: Live state of the print bridge and the connected thermal printer.
dashboard.printerUnreachableDesc: TCP {{host}}:{{port}} did not answer. Check the VLAN/firewall path and that the printer is powered on.
dashboard.lastJobDesc: Heartbeat from the bridge. Stale > 24 h usually means callers stopped, not that the printer is broken.
dashboard.noJobsYet: No jobs recorded yet — send something to /print/* or run a test print.
dashboard.recentErrorsDesc: Last 10 failed jobs. Drill into the Jobs tab for more.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.description: Print job throughput, type breakdown, and success-rate trends.
analytics.jobsOverTimeDesc: Hourly buckets up to 48 h; daily after.
analytics.byTypeDesc: Volume per endpoint over the last week.

jobs.title: Job history
jobs.description: Last 200 print jobs recorded in the local SQLite store.
jobs.tableDesc: Newest first. Source includes client IP and a truncated User-Agent.

testPrint.title: Test print
testPrint.description: Send a manual print job to validate the bridge or to fire one-off receipts.
testPrint.composerDesc: Pick a job type, fill in the fields, and hit print.
testPrint.selfTestDesc: Asks the printer to print its own status sheet (model, firmware, codepage, and on networked models the IP, MAC, subnet, gateway). Runs entirely on the printer's firmware — useful when software rendering produces blank paper, since it bypasses host encoding.
testPrint.quickDesc: Prints the same canary as the daily cron — accents, QR, and a timestamp.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Live configuration — persisted to /app/data/config.json. Takes effect on the next print job, no restart required.
settings.printerCardDesc: Network address and ESC/POS connection tuning.
settings.tokenCardDesc: Rotate the secret used by callers. The dashboard credential is updated automatically on rotation.
settings.newTokenGenerated: New token generated — save below to apply
settings.tokenRotated: Webhook token rotated — update your /print/* callers
settings.tokenMinLength: Token must be at least 24 characters
settings.languageCardDesc: Choose the interface language. Defaults to your browser preference.

setup.welcomeDesc: Bridge HTTP requests to an ESC/POS thermal printer over the network. This wizard takes about a minute and configures the printer host, an authentication token, and a verification print.
setup.welcomeItem1: Point the service at your printer's IP and raw-TCP port.
setup.welcomeItem2: Generate or paste a bearer token for the /print endpoints.
setup.welcomeItem3: Run a connectivity check and an optional test print.
setup.printerDesc: Address of your network thermal printer. Default is raw TCP / JetDirect on port 9100.
setup.tzHint: IANA name (e.g. Europe/Paris). Used for receipt timestamps.
setup.authDesc: Create your admin account, then set the bearer token webhooks will use to call the /print endpoints.
setup.authAlertDesc: This account is created in the better-auth sidecar and gets the admin role. The bearer token below is separate — it's only for webhook callers (n8n, Home Assistant, ntfy).
setup.autoDiscoverDesc: mDNS browse + parallel TCP probe of the local /24 on port 9100.
setup.noPrintersFound: No printers found on the local network
setup.tokenGenerated: Token generated — copy it somewhere safe
setup.tokenHint: Use at least 24 characters. A 64-char hex string is recommended.
setup.verifyDesc: TCP-probe the printer before saving the configuration.
setup.printerUnreachableMsg: Printer is unreachable — check IP, port, and VLAN
setup.cannotReachDesc: You can still save and finish setup. The service will keep retrying when print requests come in. Check the host/port and any firewall or VLAN restrictions on port 9100.
setup.openPrinterWebUIHint: Use the open icon to load the printer's manufacturer web UI in a new tab (port 80) — handy to find or change the IP.
setup.doneDesc: Review the summary, then save the configuration. The service stores it in /app/data and applies it immediately — no restart needed.

public.title: Print something
public.description: Send a message or a drawing straight to the thermal printer.
public.composeDesc: Type a message or draw, then hit print.
public.drawDesc: Draw with your finger or mouse. The canvas is printed centered.

draw.title: Draw your dessert
draw.description: Sketch your dessert with your finger and send it straight to the thermal printer.
draw.cardDesc: Black ink on a white background — that's what looks best on 80 mm thermal paper.
draw.caption: Caption (optional)
